degraded: F0 suite RED on ONE pre-existing, foreign failure: test_architecture_md_reflects_arch_impact flags a decision-drop belonging to iterate-2026-07-18-fr-existence-gate (gitignored drops resolve from the MAIN root, so a concurrent iterate's drop reddens this worktree). The run_id in the failure is not this run's - inherited, not introduced.

The triage card trg-0ce59c05 asserted 'VERIFIED ALREADY CORRECT (do not redo)' for shared/templates/. That premise was FALSE in two of three respects (setup-uv unpinned in the shipped template while the monorepo pins it for itself; AikidoSec/github-actions-wf 404s entirely). Scope was widened with explicit user approval to fix the state, because the requested guard would otherwise have been red on its own PR.

The CI supply-chain ack gate does NOT fire for this diff: CI_SUPPLYCHAIN_FILE_PATTERNS matches only this repo's .github/**, while the diff edits shipped templates under shared/templates/. An edit to a shipped CI template changes every future adopter's trust boundary but escapes the ack. Filed as its own card; the concrete drift mode is blocked by the new hard guard.

No hosted dependency-updater config (Dependabot OR Renovate) is shipped to adopters
The posture rule reaches claude-md-template.md with all five normative claims intact

test_completeness.enumeration_basis: Every behavior introduced by this diff, grouped into 7 rows over 28 tests. No 'untestable' rows: all behaviour is a text assertion over tracked files. Both guard directions were driven RED on a mutated real template and restored from git, so the gate is proven on the bug it exists to catch rather than asserted. Rows 6-7 exist because the external review found the first-cut parser silently skipped quoted and dynamic `uses:` values - they evaded BOTH direction checks while the non-vacuity count still passed.
